headline: The first 48-team World Cup — 12 groups, 104 matches, a brand-new format
summary: WC 2026 is the first 48-team World Cup, expanded from 32. Teams are split into 12 groups of four; the top two from each group plus the eight best third-placed teams advance to a new Round of 32. Total matches jump from 64 to 104, and the tournament runs 39 days. It's the first format change since 1998, and the expansion remains debated — more upsets and global representation versus more lopsided mismatches.
angles: 48 teams, 104 matches — is a bigger World Cup better or watered down?
